author: @christianpnorris
text: As if there weren't enough byproducts from oil refining to make enough plastic to clog the ocean... NOW for some reason it's deemed frickin awesome to make more from trees?  One hand clapping.

replies.author: @AnalystPrime
replies.text: These materials are biodegradable, at least the other tree products are. This is nowhere near the first time someone realized there are far more forests than oil fields and made plastic replacement out of sawmill waste.

author: @PeaceJourney...
text: Is the bark itself converted to plastic? I remember in the 90s we had a company hereabouts that combined waste cedar bits with recycling plastic and then extruded building materials with the results. Made window sashes, boardwalks and piers.

replies.author: @__WJK__
replies.text: And let me guess, not a single independent/3rd party study was conducted to test for long-term toxicity impacts on the environment, as the product ages.  Point being, it would be much better to nip potential problems in the bud, before they turn into the next asbestos, lead paint, or leaded gas debacle.

replies.author: @PeaceJourney...
replies.text:
@__WJK__ your guess is as good as mine. Good point. I merely was pointing out this is not new tech, it's been around since the 80s. 
You be you Boo.

author: @baby-turtle
text: Just another idiotic idea to destroy more forests...

replies.author: @baby-turtle
replies.text: @Warp9pnt9 well... If everything recyclable was actually recycled we'd all be better off... Turning what would otherwise be considered waste into something useful is definitely good but, waste products should be used where they are most suitable to be used... There are multiple and much better uses for wood "waste" than turning it into plastic, and there are many other alternatives that are much simpler to make using crops that grow back every year. Trees grow too slow to be a really viable option.

replies.author: @ElizabethGreene
replies.text: Tree bark is currently a waste product that is thrown away.  Using waste for something useful is usually considered a good thing.

replies.author: @baby-turtle
replies.text: @ElizabethGreene The time, energy, and materials that go into the process is the problem. Tree bark as mulch, top soil mix, and even MDF or OSB are all much better uses and very low cost to production.

author: @__WJK__
text: Can someone please tell me why we're so focused on greenhouse gasses, while we continue to overlook the environmental toxicity timebomb that plastics are creating(?)  Putting all our eggs into greenhouse gas reduction, is hardly going to matter when biological life/DNA synthesis succumbs to disease-related plastic toxicity.

replies.author: @AnalystPrime
replies.text:
Because greenhouse gases altering the global climate and potentially killing large portion of sealife due to changing water temperatures messing up salinity, acidity, oxygen levels and changing the ocean currents is a bigger and more immediate problem.
Cleaning up garbage is far easier project and it will not kill us off in the near term.

replies.author: @__WJK__
replies.text: @AnalystPrime - Interesting, so are you suggesting more people are dying from climate change, than are dying from environmental pollution-related diseases...per year(?)

replies.author: @AnalystPrime
replies.text:
@__WJK__ Millions die each year of air pollution which is related to greenhouse gases. Droughts and floods caused by climate change destroy infrastructure and food crops and cause refugees to flood the more fortunate areas, straining their economy and causing conflicts. There are already three million such refugees in USA and over 110 million worldwide.

Heatstroke can kill people on its own but higher temperatures alone weaken them, lowering both their ability to work and therefore earn money for food and medicines, and their general health. Heat also causes rain, which causes floods, which bring disease and parasites that will kill higher number of people who have been weakened by  the other effects of climate change.

And that is nothing compared to what will happen if and when the ocean currents moving heat northwards will stop or change direction and we get another mini ice age there while the areas around equator will have superstorms every week.

Cleaning up polluted land and picking up plastic garbage from the sea is peanuts compared to what we need to do to just lower the effects of climate change.

author: @__WJK__
text: Can someone please tell me why we're so focused on greenhouse gasses, while we continue to overlook the environmental toxicity timebomb that plastics are creating(?)  Putting all our eggs into greenhouse gas reduction, is hardly going to matter when biological life/DNA synthesis succumbs to disease-related plastic toxicity.

replies.author: @ElizabethGreene
replies.text: Could you elaborate on the plastic toxicity?  One of the problems with plastic is it's too unreactive.  That makes it hard to break down, and also makes it relatively non-toxic.

author: @shadetreemechanicracing22
text: I remember a cartoon from probably the 40's they would take a whole tree and machine it down to one toothpick.

replies.author: @AnalystPrime
replies.text: IIRC that was Marvin the Martian trying to colonize Bugs Bunny's ancestral lands, only things missing were Marvin asking US senate to send in the marines to get rid of the rabbit and then being subsidized by taxpayers because his toothpick factory is not profitable enough.
